metadata.purpose: Data-driven rules that turn a routed opportunity into an INTERNAL action plan: what the owner does internally, who they coordinate with, why, and what each person prepares — before the customer engagement. Generic and reusable: role labels are canonical, and the concrete internal people (names/emails) always come from the routing config at runtime, never from this file. No company, product, person, score, or message literal belongs here.

sales: commercial progression and the business case

routed_reason.sales: You own the commercial motion for this account, so the opportunity strategy and customer alignment are yours to drive.
routed_reason.technical: You own the technical validation for this account, so the architecture and proof-of-value boundaries are yours to shape.
routed_reason.leadership: You own strategic sponsorship for this account, so the investment path and executive alignment are yours to steer.

technical_trigger.purpose: When the sales owner should coordinate with the technical owner: the customer next step requires architecture / validation / integration work that a specialist must shape.

executive_trigger.purpose: When either owner should coordinate with an executive sponsor: an exec-sponsored program is in play, or the economic authority is DISTRIBUTED across a committee/board (so the investment path needs senior alignment). An economic buyer that is merely unconfirmed early in discovery does NOT trigger this — that would make the exec loop-in constant noise.

technical: architecture boundaries and required data sources
two proof-of-value scenarios and success criteria
the top technical risks to de-risk before the session
sales: customer and executive alignment for the session
commercial framing and the business case
executive: a one-page investment case for the approvers

coordinate_reason.sales_to_technical: The customer next step needs architecture and proof-of-value boundaries defined before it is credible.
coordinate_reason.technical_to_sales: They own customer alignment and commercial progression, so the validation ladders into a real commercial motion.
coordinate_reason.to_executive_distributed: No single economic buyer — funding is committee/board-approved, so a sales leader can unblock it with peer-level air cover.
coordinate_reason.to_executive_program: It's part of an exec-sponsored program, so a sales leader keeps it aligned and funded as it advances.

executive_when: only when it reaches the funding decision

customer_roles.champion: owns the internal recommendation and coordinates the customer participants
customer_roles.economic_buyer: controls the budget for the decision
customer_roles.executive_sponsor: owns strategic alignment on the customer side

suggested_roles.purpose: Canonical, public-safe roles the AI layer (Circuit) may PROPOSE as additional internal coordination when the conversation clearly warrants it (e.g. redlines → legal, a named competitor → product specialist, a deployment-timeline condition → delivery lead). Advisory only — these never replace the deterministic sales/technical/executive routing, are role-only (never a named person), and must be grounded in the conversation.
